conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering takeout because it's just easier.
When you feel like you can't do something you used to manage, like cooking, what goes through your mind?
I just think I’m getting old. I look at people my age and they're out there doing things, and I'm just stuck in this rut.
When you compare yourself to others your age, what does that "rut" feel like?
Like I'm missing out. They're building lives and I'm just existing. It’s a really lonely feeling.
That lonely feeling of just existing—what does that do to your sense of purpose?
It makes me feel like I’m just waiting for something to happen, but I don't know what that is anymore.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s like I'm just going through the motions. I wake up, I sleep, I eat, but I don't really live anymore.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
Maybe a year ago? I used to have goals and plans, but now I just feel like I'm just drifting.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Everything. I look at my phone and I see everyone posting about their lives, and I realize I don't have one anymore.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
Honestly, I don't see much changing. I'll just keep existing until I figure out what to do with the rest of my life.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about the past, does it make it harder to look forward to things during the day?
Yeah, it does. I just keep thinking about what could have gone wrong, and it makes me feel like I’m stuck in this loop where I can’t move forward.
You mentioned feeling stuck in a loop—has that made it harder to get started on things you normally enjoy?
Yeah, completely. I used to love painting, but now I just can’t seem to pick up the brush. It feels like too much effort when I’m just going to end up staring at the canvas.
When you say this will end soon, are you talking about ending your life?
I don't know. I just know I can't keep going on like this. It’s exhausting trying to be happy when you feel like you're failing at everything.
Have you thought about specific steps or a specific way you would do it?
I have thought about it, but I don't want to do it. I just... I don't want to be that person who can't handle it.
Do you currently have access to what you would use?
I have it, but I don't want to use it. I just don't want to be that person who... you know.
What has stopped you so far from acting on these thoughts?
I don't know. I guess I just keep thinking that someone will come and figure it out before it’s too late.
You said this might happen soon - what has made things feel this immediate right now?
I don't know. It just feels like... it feels like the clock is ticking, and I don't know how much longer I can keep going like this.
When these thoughts get strongest, what usually triggers them?
It’s usually when I’m alone. I’ll be sitting in the apartment, and suddenly I just feel the weight of it all.
Have these thoughts been constant lately, or do they come in waves?
They come in waves. Sometimes I'll be fine for a few hours, and then suddenly I just start thinking about it again.
What has helped you hold on, even a little, when those thoughts show up?
Just knowing that someone might come by and find me. I don't want to be the one who... I don't want anyone to have to deal with that.
